summary: iter266.11 Labor Brain Advisory Rail backend testing — all 8 pytest cases PASS against the public preview URL. Covered: envelope shape + max-5 ranked-by-severity-weight ordering, outlet & department scoping, accept->PAF write, idempotency on (rec_id, day), decisions audit feed, Pydantic validation on accept, plus regression on /dashboard (incl. labor_pct_of_sales) and /shifts.
critical_code_review_comments: echo_schedule.py is approaching 813 lines; consider splitting schedule_dashboard, _generate_labor_brain_recs, and accept handlers into separate modules (e.g., routes/echo_schedule/dashboard.py, /labor_brain.py).

labor_brain endpoint internally calls schedule_dashboard(...) directly as a Python function — works because both share the router; if /dashboard ever gets request-bound deps (auth, headers), this in-process call will silently bypass them. Acceptable today, but document it.

DB writes in /labor-brain/accept swallow all exceptions with bare `except Exception: pass`. If the insert truly fails, the API still returns ok=true with a paf that was never persisted. Recommend logging the exception (logger.exception) at minimum.
Idempotency key is `labor-brain::{rec_id}::{YYYY-MM-DD}` in UTC. Cross-midnight UTC retries will mint a new PAF for the same rec; acceptable per spec but worth a UI hint.
seed_data_creation: Inserts TEST_-prefixed rec_ids into borrow_pafs + labor_brain_decisions for idempotency verification. Natural keys are date-scoped so they expire/rotate naturally; not destructive.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Run `pytest /app/backend/tests/test_iter266_11_labor_brain.py -v` for a fast smoke. Test inserts use uuid-based TEST_ rec_ids so reruns on the same day are still net-new. /api/echo-schedule/labor-brain depends on /dashboard tile aggregation — if /dashboard regresses, every labor-brain test will cascade. Frontend (LaborBrainRail in /app/client/modules/Schedule/index.tsx) was NOT exercised per request.
